intro: Naphtali (meaning my wrestling), sixth son of Jacob and second son of Bilhah, Rachel's handmaid, was born after Rachel declared, "I have wrestled greatly with my sister and have prevailed" (Gen. 30:8). He had four sons when the family descended to Egypt (Gen. 46:24). Jacob's blessing describes him as "a doe let loose" who "gives beautiful words" (Gen. 49:21). The tribe of Naphtali received territory in the far north of Canaan around the Sea of Galilee and west of the upper Jordan. The region "Naphtali" later became synonymous with Galilee of the nations — the territory where Jesus began his Galilean ministry, fulfilling Isaiah's prophecy (Isa. 9:1–2; Matt. 4:15–16).
